variable.title: Variables
variable.searchPlaceholder: Search by variable ID or name

variable.readOnlyOnRunDesc: During pipeline execution, variable values cannot be reset via ::set-variable, etc.
variable.showOnExecDesc: Variables set as input parameters can have their values modified in scenarios such as manual triggering, remote triggering, openapi, and sub-pipeline calls.
variable.idRule: Variable ID must start with a letter or underscore and can only contain letters, numbers, and underscores
variable.constantIdRule: Constant ID must be all uppercase, start with a letter or underscore, and can only contain uppercase letters, numbers, and underscores
variable.idPlaceholder: Please enter variable ID, e.g., my_variable
variable.constantIdPlaceholder: Please enter constant ID, e.g., MAX_COUNT
variable.deleteConfirmContent: Are you sure you want to delete the variable "{name}"? This action cannot be undone.
variable.loadPluginVariablesFailed: Failed to load plugin output variables
variable.variableTips: Variables can be referenced using the expression ${'{{'}variables.<var_name>{'}}'}
variable.pluginOutputVariableTips: Plugin output variables can be referenced using the expression ${'{{'}steps.<step_id>.outputs.<output_name>{'}}'}
variable.systemVariableTips: System variables can be referenced using the expression ${'{{'}ci.<var_name>{'}}'}
variable.apiUrlPlaceholder: Enter API URL, supports {'{'} variableName {'}'} for variable reference
variable.apiUrlTips: Supports using {'{'} variableName {'}'} format to reference other variable values
variable.dataPathPlaceholder: e.g.: data.list, leave empty to use response data
variable.batchImportRule1: Separate multiple options with line breaks
variable.batchImportRule2: Similar to the HTML select tag, options support value=name / value formats
variable.batchImportPlaceholder: Please break your lines if there are several things to be recorded
variable.fetchFailed: Failed to fetch data, please check API configuration
variable.apiUrlTipsWithVar: Support using {'{'} variableName {'}'} to reference other variable values, e.g.: /api/options?type={'{'} type {'}'}
variable.dependencyNotResolved: Dependent variable value is empty, cannot fetch data

labelGroup.title: Label Management
labelGroup.hint: Note: You can add up to 10 label groups; a single group can have up to 12 labels; a label can contain up to 20 characters

labelGroup.tagNamePlaceholder: Please enter the label name, no more than 20 characters
labelGroup.deleteGroupConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this label group?
labelGroup.deleteTagConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this label?
labelGroup.desc: Assigning reasonable labels to creation flows helps in quickly locating and managing creation flows. Please be aware of the following constraints:
labelGroup.contentOne: 1. Up to 10 groups can be added under a single project
labelGroup.contentTwo: 2. Up to 12 labels can be added under a single group

actions.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete "{name}"?

delegation.tips1: The following scenarios will use the delegate's permissions:
delegation.tips2: 1. In scenarios such as cloud desktop event triggers, scheduled triggers, and sub-creation flow triggers, the platform will verify if the delegate has {0}; if not, the trigger will fail.
delegation.tips3: 2. During creation flow execution, the delegate will be assigned to the built-in variable {0}, and some plugins will verify whether there is corresponding system/function operation permission based on this data.
delegation.tips4: Please clarify the functions and scope of operations of the creation flow before authorizing the delegate to avoid unauthorized access.
delegation.tips5: The default delegate is the creator of the creation flow; if you need to change it, please {0} reset it here.
delegation.resetAuthTips1: After resetting, scenarios such as repository triggers, scheduled triggers, and sub-creation flow triggers will use {0} to execute the creation flow.
delegation.resetAuthTips2: Please confirm whether authorization is possible.

execute.addRemarkForBuild: Add a remark for this build
execute.constraintModeDesc: Constraint Mode, controlled by the template
execute.stopSuc: Build termination request sent successfully, please wait...
execute.reBuildTips1: When rebuilding, it will execute again with the same trigger parameters, and:
execute.reBuildTips3: - The creation flow version used is based on the first trigger
execute.rePlayTips1: After replay, a new build will be started with the same trigger parameters:
execute.rePlayTips3: - Runs using the latest creation flow version
execute.reBuildConfirmTips: Are you sure you want to initiate a rebuild?
execute.rePlayConfirmTips: Are you sure you want to replay the event for this creation flow?
execute.rePlayInfo1: 1. During replay, a new build is started with the same trigger parameters and the latest creation flow orchestration.
execute.rePlayInfo2: 2. During replay, trigger detection is skipped and may not meet the trigger conditions of the latest version.
execute.reBuildInfo1: 1. Rebuilding re-executes on the basis of the previous build.
execute.reBuildInfo2: 2. Creation flow orchestration version, BK_CI_BUILD_ID, and first trigger are consistent.

permission.exitContactAdmin: Obtained permissions through user group, if you need to exit, please contact the project administrator to exit the user group first
